steps.title: Similar triangles have equal ratios
steps.content: Both trees are measured at the same time, so the sun's angle is identical. This creates similar right triangles for both trees, meaning their height-to-shadow ratios must be equal. For tree 1: $$\frac{10}{5} = 2$$

steps.title: Backsolve for tree 2's height
steps.content: Backsolving — testing choice C (8): $$\frac{8}{2} = 4 \neq 2$$. Fails. Testing choice B (4): $$\frac{4}{2} = 2$$, matching tree 1's ratio. That matches choice B — B is correct.
